macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Fed seeks input on proposed ‘skinny’ accounts as crypto policy debate continues
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Reserve is soliciting feedback on proposed low-cost ‘skinny’ accounts, part of a broader payments and financial-access discussion that overlaps with digital asset and stablecoin policy. In the same news cycle, South Korea’s proposed crypto tax faced mounting public opposition, with a petition surpassing the signature threshold for government review.

items.title: MoneyGram deepens blockchain payments push with Tempo partnership
items.summary: MoneyGram has partnered with payments blockchain Tempo to support stablecoin settlement and validate remittance transactions on the network. The move highlights continued adoption of blockchain infrastructure for cross-border payments and treasury flows.

items.title: Euro stablecoin consortium Qivalis expands to 37 banks
items.summary: Qivalis, the European banking consortium building a regulated euro stablecoin, added 25 banks and now includes 37 member institutions across 15 countries. The group is targeting a launch in the second half of 2026.

items.title: Blockchain.com files for U.S. IPO as crypto firms test public markets
items.summary: Blockchain.com has filed for a U.S. IPO with the SEC, signaling renewed appetite among major crypto companies to access public equity markets. The filing adds to a growing wave of tokenization and infrastructure firms exploring listings.

items.title: Securitize plans SPAC merger to go public and expand tokenization platform
items.summary: Tokenization platform Securitize is planning a SPAC merger to become a public company, aiming to scale its securities tokenization business. The deal reflects rising institutional interest in real-world asset tokenization and blockchain-based capital markets infrastructure.

items.title: U.S. lawmakers advance Bitcoin reserve legislation
items.summary: A U.S. lawmaker unveiled a bill to codify a strategic Bitcoin reserve, drawing bipartisan attention and adding fresh momentum to the policy debate around government-held digital assets. The proposal underscores how Bitcoin is increasingly intersecting with national reserve and fiscal policy discussions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic reportedly resets AI expectations with new model and business momentum
items.summary: Recent coverage highlights Anthropic’s strong week, including reports that Andrej Karpathy is joining the company, new financial estimates suggesting the lab may already be profitable, and a deepening compute partnership with SpaceX. The developments are being framed as a major signal for the broader AI race and for how investors are valuing frontier model labs.

items.title: Anthropic-related headlines point to major funding and valuation speculation
items.summary: Another recent discussion centered on Anthropic’s surge in attention, including talk of a possible $900 billion valuation and a large war chest for AI infrastructure and training. The segment also touched on the broader public-market rebound in tech and the cost dynamics of AI tokens.

items.title: Google announces Flutter 3.44 and Dart 3.12 with new AI-focused tooling
items.summary: Google unveiled updates to Flutter and Dart, emphasizing AI-driven app experiences, improved hot reload behavior with coding agents, and new integrations that make it easier to call Gemini models directly from apps. The release also expands tooling around Firebase and performance improvements for cross-platform development.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung to livestream K-Pop concerts monthly on its TVs for free
items.summary: Samsung is adding a recurring free K-Pop concert livestream experience across its TV lineup, expanding its entertainment content strategy. The move underscores how TV makers are using exclusive programming to differentiate their platforms.

items.title: Meta quietly launches a dedicated Facebook Groups app called Forum
items.summary: Meta has released a standalone app for Facebook Groups, suggesting a push to make group interactions easier to access outside the main Facebook app. The launch appears to be a low-key product update rather than a major announcement.

items.title: Google replaces another major piece of Fitbit’s identity
items.summary: Google continues reshaping Fitbit’s product identity, replacing another prominent Fitbit element as it integrates the brand more tightly into its own ecosystem. The change is another sign of Fitbit’s gradual transition under Google ownership.

items.title: Google announces AI-powered Ask YouTube search feature
items.summary: Google is introducing a new search capability for YouTube that uses its AI systems to answer user questions and surface relevant video content. The feature highlights Google’s broader push to add generative search experiences across its products.

items.title: HMD bundles Indian AI chatbot onto new smartphone to expand locally
items.summary: Finnish phone maker HMD is partnering with an Indian AI chatbot provider as it targets the local market with a new smartphone. The move reflects rising localization and software partnerships in the mobile industry.

items.title: Google triples Gemini usage limits for Antigravity
items.summary: Google has significantly increased Gemini usage limits for Antigravity, giving users more room to interact with the service. The change points to Google adjusting capacity and adoption incentives for its AI products.

items.title: Microsoft continues Copilot rollback in Office with toolbar button move
items.summary: Microsoft is rolling back part of its Copilot integration by allowing users to move the button back to the ribbon in Office. The update reflects ongoing user feedback around interface changes in Microsoft 365 apps.

items.title: Apple releases Safari Technology Preview 244 with fixes and performance improvements
items.summary: Apple has issued a new Safari Technology Preview build focused on bug fixes and performance tuning. The release offers an early look at upcoming browser improvements before they reach the mainstream Safari browser.

items.title: Anker debuts earbuds with new AI chip for stronger noise reduction
items.summary: Anker has launched earbuds featuring a new chip designed to improve active noise reduction performance. The product is part of a broader wave of consumer audio upgrades centered on better processing and battery efficiency.

items.title: Smart ring maker Oura files confidentially for a U.S. IPO
items.summary: Oura has confidentially filed for a U.S. initial public offering, signaling a major corporate milestone for the wearable maker. The move suggests the company is preparing for a public-market debut amid continued demand for health-focused wearables.
